growth_records.agit: 170 rpm stirring (shaker KS 4000 i, IKA) for batch cultivations

up.method: Table 1: trehalose mode consumes trehalose+pyruvate; glucose mode consumes glucose+pyruvate+glutamate (rates modeled only)

sec.method: Table 1: produces acetate and lactate (no numeric flux reported)

growth_records.cite: Garza et al. Emergence of alternative states in a synthetic human gut microbial community. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-67036-5. [PMC12789478]
growth_records.snippet: Control samples converge to a steady state in which Blautia hydrogenotrophica, Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron, and Roseburia intestinalis coexist ... feed delivered at an approximate rate of 0.04 h−1.
growth_records.notes: mu=D=0.04 h-1 (calculated, chemostat steady-state coexistence). doubling_time=ln2/0.04=17.3 h. B. hydrogenotrophica switches between trehalose-consuming and glucose-consuming phenotypes (diauxic; growth rate increases during glucose phase per Fig 2D but not numerically tabulated). WC broth composition not enumerated in text (stated=false); only trehalose 0.71 mM quantified. Uptake/secretion qualitative (Table 1). Temperature/pH not stated in extractable text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 18073 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/18073

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 18073 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/18073

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 18073 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/18073
